delete.loadFailed: Couldn't load the delete details. Please try again.

board.title: Delete {{name}}?
board.subtitle: This removes the board. Issues are kept.
board.warnSprints: This board has {{count}} sprint(s) — they will be deleted too.
board.warnIssuesKept: No issues are deleted: {{count}} issue(s) return to the backlog.

project.title: Delete {{name}}?
project.subtitle: This removes the project and everything tied to it.
project.warnBoards: {{count}} board(s) (incl. {{sprints}} sprint(s)) owned by this project will be deleted.
project.warnSharedBoards: {{count}} shared board(s) will keep working — the project is just removed from them.
project.warnTeams: The project will be detached from {{count}} team(s).
project.warnArticles: {{count}} wiki article(s) in this project will be deleted.
project.deleteIssuesHint: Permanently removes them with their comments and files.
project.migrateIssuesHint: Keep the issues — choose where they go.
project.migrateNoTargets: No other project available to move them into.

team.title: Delete {{name}}?
team.subtitle: This removes the team and its memberships.
team.warnAccess: {{members}} member(s) will lose access to this team and the {{projects}} project(s), {{boards}} board(s) and {{issues}} issue(s) it grants.
team.warnKept: Projects, boards and issues are not deleted.

errors.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid e-mail address

emailReply.sendFailed: The reply couldn't be sent. Please try again.

connect.title: Connect to your server
connect.subtitle: Hinata is self-hosted. Enter the URL of your organization's Hinata server to get started.
connect.invalidUrl: Please enter a valid http(s) URL
connect.failed: Could not connect to this server. Please verify the URL.

server.switchHint: Switch the server this app connects to.
server.addSubtitle: Enter the URL of another Hinata server to connect to.
server.removeConfirm: Forget "{{name}}"? Its saved sign-in on this device will be cleared.
server.manageSubtitle: Choose the server this app connects to.
server.notReachableHint: Check the address and that the server is online. A TLS certificate or port could be blocking.

setup.title: Set up your server
setup.subtitle: This server hasn't been configured yet. Create the organization and the first administrator account.

onboarding.tagline: Where your team finds the light.
onboarding.label: Feature

projects.title: Projects
projects.body: Organize work and track issues on your own terms — with custom workflows per project.

sprints.title: Sprints
sprints.body: Plan focused two-week cycles. See exactly where your team stands — in real time.

teams.title: Teams
teams.body: See who's working on what. Stay in sync without the noise.

time.title: Time Tracking
time.body: Monitor and report time spent on different projects and various activity types.

gantt.title: Gantt Charts
gantt.body: Create and update your project plans on an interactive timeline with dependencies.

timesheets.title: Timesheets
timesheets.body: Track work activity for yourself and the members of your team, week by week.

boards.title: Agile Boards
boards.body: Plan and prioritize your development on a board that mirrors your team's process.

reports.title: Reports
reports.body: Collect and summarize insightful bits of information about your projects.

dashboards.title: Dashboards
dashboards.body: Monitor activity in all of your projects at a glance — tasks, progress and performance.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.body: Build a collection of articles that cover everything your team needs to know.

auth.subtitle: Sign in to continue
auth.tooManyAttempts: Too many failed attempts. Please try again later.
auth.twoFactorSubtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app, or a recovery code.
auth.ssoOnlyBody: Password sign-in is disabled for this server. Continue with a provider below.
auth.ssoOnlyNoProviders: Password sign-in is disabled and no single sign-on provider is configured yet. Please contact your administrator.

update.title: Update required

comments.deleteSelectedBody: This permanently removes it for everyone.
comments.deleteSelectedBody_plural: This permanently removes {{count}} comments for everyone.

issues.noDescription: No description yet. Double-tap to add one.
issues.editTitleHint: Double-tap the title or description to edit.
issues.title: Title
issues.addComment: Write a comment… type @ to link
issues.deleteBody: {{id}} will be permanently deleted, including comments and work items.
issues.archiveBody: {{id}} will be archived and hidden from boards and lists. It can be restored at any time.
issues.deleteCommentBody: This comment will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.
issues.author: Author
issues.label: Label

attachments.removeConfirm: Remove this file? This can't be undone.

replyEmail.title: Reply by email
replyEmail.subtitle: Send an email reply to the original sender of this ticket.

projects.title: Projects
projects.empty: No projects yet. Create the first one!
projects.name: Project name
projects.keyHelp: Short code used for issue IDs, e.g. ASTA → ASTA-42
projects.newSubtitle: Set it up now — tune workflow & labels in settings after.
projects.defaultWorkflowInfo: Starts with the default workflow — Backlog → Open → In Progress → In Review → Done (resolved: Done) — editable any time in Project settings.

board.title: Agile Boards
board.name: Board name
board.emptyProject: No boards for this project yet.
board.timelineEmpty: Nothing to show on the timeline.
board.createSub: Set it up now, fill it from the backlog after.
board.typeKanbanDesc: Continuous flow — no fixed timeboxes. Pull tasks across columns one after another.
board.typeScrumDesc: Project management in fixed sprints (2–4 weeks). Plan work from the backlog into each sprint.
board.renameSubtitle: Give this board a new name.

gantt.title: Timeline
gantt.subtitle: Gantt view with dependencies

reports.empty: No data for this report yet.

knowledge.title: Knowledge base
knowledge.subtitle: {{articles}} articles · {{spaces}} spaces
knowledge.empty: No articles yet. Share your team's knowledge!
knowledge.writeHint: Write in markdown… type @ to link an issue, article or teammate.
knowledge.noResultsMessage: Try another term or create a new article.
knowledge.newSpaceSubtitle: Group related articles into a space.
knowledge.deleteSpaceConfirm: Delete the “{{name}}” space? This can’t be undone.

weeklySummary.title: Weekly Summary
weeklySummary.allCaughtUpMessage: You have no open to-dos right now. Enjoy the calm!

search.placeholder: Search issues, projects, people…  or type a command

empty.title: Search anything in Hinata
empty.subtitle: Find issues, projects, people, boards and docs — or run a command. Start typing.

search.noMatchSub: Try a different term, an issue id like HIV-241, or a command name.

settings.title: Settings
settings.adminAreaDesc: Manage the workspace, users, SSO and more.

admin.title: Admin area
admin.subtitle: Settings take effect immediately — no restart required.
admin.localizationHint: Default timezone and language for new users.
admin.ssoHint: Connect your identity provider (e.g. Synology SSO via OpenID Connect or SAML).
admin.oidcSubtitle: OpenID Connect — recommended for Synology, Keycloak, Authentik
admin.smtpHint: Used for all transactional e-mails (issue notifications, invites).
admin.emailIngestHint: Polls an IMAP mailbox and converts new e-mails into issues automatically.
admin.imapSubtitle: IMAP — polls inbox and creates issues from incoming mail
admin.pushHint: Mobile push via Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM).
admin.fcmSubtitle: Requires a Firebase project with a service account JSON key.
admin.fcmDocHint: Download the service account JSON from Firebase console → Project settings → Service accounts.
admin.gitHint: Register Hinata as an OAuth app once per provider. Every project then connects its own repository through it with a single click — this is platform-wide setup, not per project.
admin.gitProvidersHint: Register an OAuth app per provider so users can connect a repository in one click. Without credentials, users connect via a repository URL + access token instead.
admin.gitWebhookHint: The public callback/webhook base URL, and the key that protects stored access tokens.
admin.gitStatusLiveHint: Real OAuth — client credentials are configured.
admin.gitStatusDemoHint: Not configured — add a client ID and secret to enable one-click OAuth.
admin.gitWebhookUrlHint: Your public /api/v1 URL, used for the OAuth callback and webhooks — e.g. https://track.example.com/api/v1. Register <base>/git/oauth/callback as the provider app's callback URL.
admin.gitTokenSecretHint: At least 16 characters — encrypts stored provider tokens at rest.
admin.gitTokenSecretWarning: Changing this makes already-connected repositories unreadable; they must be reconnected.
admin.gitTokenSecretSet: A custom secret is in effect.
admin.gitTokenSecretDefault: Using the built-in default — set your own before going live.
admin.bruteForceHint: Locks accounts temporarily after repeated failed login attempts.
admin.rateLimitSubtitle: Limits API requests per IP to prevent abuse.
admin.sessionPolicyHint: How long users stay signed in without activity.
admin.owaspNote: Hinata enforces OWASP Top 10 protections by default. The settings above allow further hardening for your specific environment.
admin.auditLogHint: Every admin action and login event will be listed here with timestamps and actor details.
admin.secretHint: Leave blank to keep the stored value
admin.deleteConfirmBody: This permanently deletes the account and revokes all access. Assigned issues will be unassigned. This action cannot be undone.
admin.deleteFinalBody: To confirm, type the username {{username}} below. {{name}} will be permanently deleted.
admin.appReleasesHint: Version requirements and policies served to the mobile and web apps.
admin.platformHint: Platform-conform defaults. Switch these on to unlock the flexible modes.
admin.multiAssigneeHint: Off: one person per issue. On: assign several people.
admin.emailReplyHint: Off: no email replies. On: reply to the sender of email-to-ticket issues directly from the issue.
admin.featureFlagsHint: Toggle optional client features by name. Served to apps on every start.

um.title: User management
um.subtitle: {{n}} users · roles, authentication & account lifecycle
um.expiredBanner: The invitation sent on {{date}} has expired. Re-send it to let {{name}} finish setting up their account.
um.resendInvitationSub: Email a fresh sign-up link, valid for 7 more days
um.deactivateAccountSub: Block sign-in & revoke sessions — data is kept
um.revokeAdminRightsSub: Return to a regular user role
um.passwordManagedByIdpSub: The identity provider controls credentials for this user
um.sendPasswordResetSub: Email a secure reset link to {{email}}
um.revokeAllSessionsSub: Force sign-out on every device ({{n}} active)
um.emptyMessage: Try a different search term or clear the filters.
um.inviteSub: Send sign-up links to new local accounts
um.inviteMessageHint: Add a personal note to the invitation…
um.deactivateSub: Block sign-in for the selected users
um.deactivateWarn: This will revoke {{n}} active session(s). Account data is preserved and can be restored by reactivating.
um.revokeAdminSub: Return the selected users to a regular role
um.resetSubMany: Email password reset links to {{n}} users
um.deleteWarn: The account(s) and memberships are permanently removed. Authored issues are reassigned to a former user.

admin.authHint: Control how users sign in and register.
admin.localAuthHint: Allow sign-in, sign-up and password reset with email and password. Turn off to require SSO.
admin.registrationHint: Let new users create their own account from the sign-in screen.
admin.approvalHint: New sign-ups must be approved by an admin after confirming their email.
admin.mcpHint: The embedded MCP (Model Context Protocol) server lets AI assistants act on Hinata on a user's behalf, authenticated by their Personal Access Tokens. Configure the platform-wide policy here.
admin.mcpServerHint: Identity of the embedded MCP server exposed to clients.
admin.mcpAccessHint: Control whether users can mint tokens and how many they may hold.
admin.mcpEnabledHint: When off, the MCP endpoint is closed and the token settings are hidden from users. Leave untouched to follow the server default.
admin.mcpMaxPatsHint: Leave blank to use the server default. 0 means unlimited.

ingest.emptyMessage: Connect a mailbox to turn incoming e-mails into tickets — each connection can feed a different project.
ingest.name: Name (optional)
ingest.folderHint: Enter the exact folder name (e.g. INBOX) — it must match the mailbox exactly. Or scan the mailbox to pick from its folders.
ingest.scanConsentBody: The server will connect to {{host}} with the entered credentials and list the mailbox's folders. Your e-mails are neither read nor changed.
ingest.scanNeedsCredentials: Enter host, e-mail/username and password first.
ingest.passwordStored: A password is stored — leave blank to keep it
ingest.projectRequired: Choose the project that receives the tickets.
ingest.deleteBody: "{{name}}" will no longer be polled. Existing tickets are kept.

empty.title: No audit events
empty.message: Security-relevant actions like sign-ins, role changes and settings updates will appear here.

action.MCP_KB_CREATED: Knowledge base article created via MCP
action.MCP_KB_UPDATED: Knowledge base article updated via MCP
action.MCP_KB_DELETED: Knowledge base article deleted via MCP

create.sub: Set it up now, fill it from the backlog after.

start.title: Start {{name}}
start.sub: Lock the scope and begin the timebox.

complete.title: Complete {{name}}
complete.sub: Review the outcome and re-home unfinished work.

sprint.name: Sprint name
sprint.goalHint: What outcome should this sprint deliver?
sprint.dragHere: Drag issues here to plan this sprint, or add one below.
sprint.noActiveSub: Start a sprint from the Planning tab to run the board.
sprint.noInsights: Start a sprint to see its insights.
sprint.noBurndown: Set sprint dates to see the burndown.
sprint.noBreakdown: No estimated work in this sprint.
sprint.netScope: Net scope change: {{delta}} pts since sprint start.

toast.created: “{{name}}” created — add issues from the backlog.

teams.title: Teams
teams.newHint: Group people and grant project access
teams.createSubtitle: You'll be its first Team-Admin. Add people & projects next.
teams.editSubtitle: Edit how this team appears across the workspace.
teams.deleteSubtitle: This removes the team and its memberships.
teams.deleteWarn: Projects are not deleted — they return to the workspace. {{count}} members will lose this team's access.
teams.name: Team name

access.someHint: Choose exactly what they can see
access.noneHint: Add them now, grant access later

teams.everyoneOnTeam: Everyone is already on this team.
teams.roleHintAdmin: Can manage members, projects & settings of this team.
teams.roleHintMember: Can view and work on the projects you grant below.
teams.noProjectsYet: This team has no projects yet.
teams.lastAdminHint: A team needs at least one Team-Admin — promote someone else first.
teams.addProjectSubtitle: Attach an existing project or spin up a new one for {{name}}.
teams.allProjectsAttached: Every project is already on this team.
teams.detachConfirm: Remove {{name}} from this team? Members lose access granted through this team.
teams.adminsOnlyHint: Only Team-Admins and platform administrators can change team settings.
teams.permAdmin: Full control of this team — members, projects, settings. Same rights as a platform admin, scoped to {{name}}.
teams.permMember: Work on the projects they're granted. Can't change team membership or settings.
teams.deleteThisHint: Removes the team and all memberships. Projects stay in the workspace.

projectSettings.title: Project settings
projectSettings.leadsNote: Star a member to make them a project lead. At least one lead is required.
projectSettings.labelsNote: Reusable tags for issues — rename, recolor or remove.
projectSettings.workflowNote: The columns an issue moves through. Toggle Resolved for states that count as done. Minimum 2 states and 1 resolved.
projectSettings.archiveNote: Archived projects are hidden from the active list and read-only, but never deleted.
projectSettings.deleteNote: Permanently removes the project, its boards and sprints. You decide what happens to its issues.
projectSettings.toastLeadRequired: A project needs at least one lead
projectSettings.toastResolvedRequired: Keep at least one resolved state
projectSettings.toastMinStates: A workflow needs at least 2 states
projectSettings.migrateBody: Choose a status to move the issues from "{{name}}" into — only then can it be removed.
projectSettings.migrateManualHint: You can also move the issues manually before deleting the status.

avatar.title: Profile picture
avatar.subtitle: Upload a new photo or remove the current one.

security.title: Email & security
security.subtitle: Sign-in address, password and two-factor authentication.
security.pendingEmail: Pending confirmation for {{email}}. Your current email stays active until you confirm the link.
security.ssoManaged: Email and password are managed by your identity provider ({{provider}}).
security.passwordResetHint: Reset via a one-time email link
security.twoFactorOff: Add an authenticator app for a second sign-in step

sessions.title: Active sessions
sessions.subtitle: Devices currently signed in to your account.
sessions.revokeMessage: Sign out {{device}}? It will need to sign in again.
sessions.revokeOthersMessage: Every device except this one will be signed out immediately.
sessions.revokeOthersToast: Signed out of all other devices

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Choose what reaches you, and on which channel.

mentions.label: Mentions & replies
mentions.desc: Someone @mentions you or replies to your comment

assigned.desc: You are added as the assignee of an issue

comments.label: Comments on my issues

status.label: Status changes
status.desc: Watched issues move across the board

ingest.label: New issues from e-mail
ingest.desc: An e-mail becomes a new issue in a project you're part of

sprint.label: Sprints & deadlines
sprint.desc: Sprint start / end and approaching due dates

invites.label: Team & project invites
invites.desc: You are invited to a team or project

digest.label: Weekly digest
digest.desc: A Monday summary of your workspace activity

access.title: Access
access.subtitle: Teams and projects you belong to, with your role in each.
access.noTeams: You're not in any teams yet
access.noProjects: No projects you can access yet

appearance.title: Appearance & app
appearance.subtitle: Interface language, theme and workspace info.

data.title: Data & privacy
data.subtitle: Export a copy of your data (GDPR Art. 15).
data.requestDesc: A machine-readable export emailed to you, within 24 hours.
data.requestModalMessage: We'll compile an export of your data and email you a secure download link, generated within 24 hours (GDPR Art. 15).
data.requestedToast: Data report requested — check your email

danger.title: Danger zone
danger.subtitle: Irreversible account actions.
danger.deleteDesc: Permanently erase your account and anonymise your authored work (GDPR Art. 17).

editModal.title: Edit profile
editModal.subtitle: Your username is your permanent handle and can't be changed.

emailModal.title: Change email
emailModal.subtitle: We'll send a confirmation link to the new address. Your sign-in email only changes once you confirm it.
emailModal.sentToast: Verification link sent to your new address

passwordModal.title: Reset password
passwordModal.message: We'll email a one-time link to set a new password. It expires in 30 minutes.

deleteModal.title: Delete my account
deleteModal.subtitle: This permanently erases your profile, credentials and sessions. Your authored issues & comments are anonymised. This cannot be undone.

tokens.title: Access tokens
tokens.subtitle: Personal Access Tokens for the MCP server and API access.

twofa.title: Two-factor authentication
twofa.step1: Step 1 of 3 · Scan the QR code
twofa.step3: Step 3 of 3 · Save your recovery codes
twofa.scanHint: Scan this with an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, 1Password, Authy…). Can't scan? Enter the key manually.
twofa.verifyHint: Enter the 6-digit code your authenticator app shows for hinata.
twofa.recoveryHint: Save these recovery codes somewhere safe. Each can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator. They won't be shown again.
twofa.newCodesHint: Your new recovery codes — save them now, they won't be shown again.
twofa.manageSubtitle: Generate a fresh set of 10 codes. Your current codes will stop working.
twofa.disableSubtitle: Enter a current 6-digit or recovery code to turn 2FA off.

invite.title: Set up your account
invite.subtitle: You're joining as {{email}}. Choose a password to finish.
invite.invalidBody: Please ask an administrator to send you a fresh invitation.

reset.title: Choose a new password
reset.subtitle: Set a new password for your account to finish.
reset.invalidBody: This password-reset link is missing or invalid. Please request a new one.

connect.title: Connect a repository
connect.urlTokenLink: Connect with a URL & access token
connect.awaitingHint: Approve access in the {{provider}} tab that just opened, then come back here — this updates automatically.
connect.unavailableBody: An administrator still needs to register hinata as an OAuth app for {{provider}} before you can connect with one click. This is a one-time platform setup done in Admin area → Git integration.
connect.tokenPromptBody: You can connect this project right now with a repository URL and access token — no admin setup required.
connect.authorizeSubtitle: hinata for {{provider}} will be able to:
connect.chooseOwner: Choose the {{owner}} hinata was installed on.
connect.tokenIntro: For self-managed GitLab / GitHub Enterprise / Bitbucket Data Center. Generate a personal access token with api / repo scope and paste it below. The token is stored encrypted and used server-side only.
connect.browserOpenError: Could not open the browser to authorize on {{provider}}.
connect.notCompleted: Authorization wasn't completed. Please try again.

git.title: Git integration
git.disconnectMessage: Development information and automation from this repository will stop updating. You can reconnect at any time.
git.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.
git.ctaBody: See branches, commits and pull / merge requests on every issue, create branches straight from an issue, and transition issues automatically as work is pushed. A project can link several repositories.
git.ctaOauthPrefix: OAuth for GitHub, GitLab & Bitbucket — or
git.automationDescPrefix: Move an issue automatically when work happens in
git.automationDescSuffix: ’s own workflow, so each project transitions on its own states.
git.branchCreatedWhen: A branch containing the key is created
git.commitPushedWhen: A commit referencing the key is pushed to any branch
git.prOpenedWhen: A {{pr}} that references the key is opened
git.prMergedWhen: A {{pr}} that references the key is merged
git.smartCommitsDesc: Run commands written in commit messages — comment, log time and transition issues.
git.branchNamingPrefix: The name suggested when creating a branch from an issue.
git.calloutSuffix: in branch names, commit messages and {{pr}} titles to link work to this project’s issues automatically.
git.branchApplyHeader: APPLY “GIT BRANCH” & “GIT CHECKOUT” TO A NEW BRANCH
git.tokensHint: Tokens are filled in when the branch is created. Applies to every issue in this project.
git.linkCommitsDesc: Add the issue key to your commit messages to link them here automatically.
git.emptySuffix: yet. Connect one to create branches and link commits.

register.title: Create your account
register.subtitle: Sign up to get started.
register.approvalHint: After you confirm your email, an administrator will review your account before you can sign in.
register.checkEmailBody: We've sent a confirmation link to {{email}}. Open it to activate your account.

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your email and we'll send you a link to choose a new password.
forgotPassword.sentBody: If an account exists for {{email}}, a password-reset link is on its way.

verifyEmail.successBody: You're all set — taking you to your dashboard…
verifyEmail.pendingBody: Your email is confirmed. An administrator needs to approve your account before you can sign in. We'll let you know by email.
verifyEmail.invalidBody: This confirmation link is invalid or has expired. Sign up again to receive a new one.

oauthConsent.title: Connect {{client}}
oauthConsent.subtitle: {{client}} wants to access your {{product}} account on your behalf.
oauthConsent.redirectNote: After you decide, you'll be returned to {{host}}.

expired.title: This authorization link has expired
expired.message: For your security this link is only valid for a short time. Please start the connection again from your AI client.

empty.title: No access tokens yet
empty.message: Create a token to connect an AI assistant or script to the MCP server.

revoke.title: Revoke token
revoke.message: Revoke “{{name}}”? Any client using it will immediately lose access. This cannot be undone.

delete.title: Delete token
delete.message: Permanently delete “{{name}}”? It will be removed from this list for good. This cannot be undone.

create.title: Create access token
create.subtitle: Grant a scoped token for the MCP server. The secret is shown only once.

created.title: Token created
created.subtitle: Copy your token now — you won't be able to see it again.
created.warning: This is the only time the token is shown. Store it somewhere safe; if you lose it you'll need to create a new one.
